bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started, and the opponent's first offer provides very low utility for me across all issues. Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.0), I will respond with my optimal opening offer to strongly anchor the negotiation and signal my ideal outcomes, focusing on the highest utility values for all issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent's offers provide very low utility for me on all issues, having only slightly shifted their offer on issueD so far. I will offer a small concession on issueD to explore their preferences, while keeping my most important issues at their maximum utility to protect value, resulting in an offer still heavily weighted in my favor.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.04), but the opponent has reverted to their initial, highly unfavorable offer. To encourage progress while protecting my core interests (issues A, B, and C where my weights are highest), I will make a small concession on issue E, dropping from valueB to valueA, which still yields excellent utility. This keeps my total expected utility very high while showing a willingness to find a compromise.

The current time fraction is very low (t=0.06), and the opponent has made almost identical bids with very low utility for me. To show good faith and attempt to find overlapping preferences, I am continuing to concede slightly on a less critical issue (issueB to valueB) as well as adjusting lower-weight issues (issueD to valueC and issueE to valueA), while holding firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueC). My utility remains reasonably close to my ideal point.
